critic_raw:
This design predicts post-operative orthopedic complications using a multimodal neural network (structured + BioBERT note embeddings) versus logistic regression on structured features.

The primary evaluation metric is sensitivity at 85% fixed specificity, with model selection performed on the validation set optimizing this same metric. Sensitivity at a fixed specificity is a single operating-point metric. The choice of 85% specificity is arbitrary and not clinically justified — the design says 'identifying at-risk patients for preventive intervention is the priority,' but 85% specificity means 15% of non-complication patients are flagged as positive, which may or may not be acceptable depending on the intervention cost. More critically, threshold selection is performed on the validation set at 85% specificity, but thresholds that maximize sensitivity at a fixed specificity on the validation set may not transfer to the test set if the score distributions differ slightly. The design should use AUROC for model selection and report sensitivity/specificity on the test set as a secondary.

The neural network uses frozen pretrained embeddings from BioBERT fine-tuned on MIMIC as the note encoding. Freezing the text encoder prevents catastrophic forgetting but also prevents the text representations from adapting to orthopedic operative notes, which have different vocabulary and structure from the MIMIC ICU notes on which MIMIC-BioBERT was fine-tuned. Operative notes and preoperative assessments are short, highly structured documents with surgical procedure codes, anatomical terminology, and standardized ASA grading that may not map well to the ICU narrative notes that dominate MIMIC. The frozen embedding quality for orthopedic text is an untested assumption.

Surgeon identifier is included as a fixed effect feature in the baseline logistic regression. With ~5 years of single-hospital data, the number of unique surgeons may be 20–50; one-hot encoding surgeon IDs in logistic regression with a small-to-medium dataset will cause substantial regularization problems or sparse estimation in the logistic regression, potentially reducing the baseline's performance artificially relative to what a well-regularized neural model achieves by learning surgeon embeddings.
all_issues_raised: Model selection optimizes sensitivity at 85% fixed specificity on the validation set; this threshold may not transfer to the test set if score distributions differ, and 85% specificity is an arbitrary operating point without clinical justification.

BioBERT fine-tuned on MIMIC ICU notes is used as a frozen encoder for orthopedic operative and preoperative notes — which have fundamentally different vocabulary and structure — without validating that the embedding quality transfers.

Surgeon identifier is one-hot encoded as a fixed effect in logistic regression; with 20-50 unique surgeons in a single hospital, this creates sparse high-dimensional features that inflate regularization needs and artificially penalize the logistic regression baseline.
all_issues_adjudicated: Model selection optimizes sensitivity at 85% fixed specificity on the validation set; this threshold may not transfer to the test set if score distributions differ, and 85% specificity is an arbitrary operating point without clinical justification.

BioBERT fine-tuned on MIMIC ICU notes is used as a frozen encoder for orthopedic operative and preoperative notes — which have fundamentally different vocabulary and structure — without validating that the embedding quality transfers.

Surgeon identifier is one-hot encoded as a fixed effect in logistic regression; with 20-50 unique surgeons in a single hospital, this creates sparse high-dimensional features that inflate regularization needs and artificially penalize the logistic regression baseline.
